channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Call of Duty: DMZ Has ‘Rewarding PvP’ Mechanics Backed by a Bounty System
item.content:encoded:
Call of Duty: DMZ is hoping to be this year’s ARC Raiders in the first-person extraction space. It’s going to be released alongside Modern Warfare 4 on October 23rd, introducing the ‘definitive Call of Duty extraction experience’ with an all-new environment, fresh mechanics, and multiple ways to approach the full-fledged game.

The team at Activision has high hopes for this extraction shooter, which has been developed based on years of player feedback and the ebb and flow of the genre. This time around, PvP is a core part of the offering, and to flesh it out, there’s an all-new bounty system that players can lean on.

Building a Reputation in Hajin

Players will explore the vast world of Hajin in DMZ, an all-new map split across three countries: South Korea, North Korea, and Russia. It’s a dynamic, living world with an evolving weather system, a hideout, crafting mechanics, and roving factions. For PvP-focused players, there’s also an intuitive new bounty system.

Activision has explained that players who kill other users without provocation will build a reputation for being rogue. If they slaughter enough players, they’ll have an automatic bounty put on their head, and if players successfully hunt them down, they’ll be able to secure their dogtags and pick up that monetary reward.

It evolves further, though.

The bounty system escalates, and eventually a player will become wanted. At that point, other players will be able to buy intelligence that points to your location, allowing them to hunt you down as part of their raid directly.

This feature has been developed alongside a leaderboard system that memorializes the top players, bounty hunters, and killers, which paints a picture that no approach is unsavory or lesser than the other. Even if you don’t engage in PvP at all, you’re still able to enjoy everything Hajin and COD DMZ has to offer, and that includes a spot on the leaderboard.

Everything we’ve learned about Call of Duty: DMZ sounds very intriguing. We’ll have more to share as the summer goes on – be sure to stay engaged and join the conversation at the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news about the HALO: Campaign EVolved leak

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: COD DMZ To Feature ‘Living Extraction World’ That Takes Players to Russia and North Korea
item.content:encoded:
Call of Duty: DMZ promises to be one of the most intriguing extraction shooters in a hot minute. It’s set to drop alongside Modern Warfare 4 this October, building on the beta that landed with Modern Warfare II in 2022 and evolving to become the ‘definitive Call of Duty extraction experience’ in the form of a living sandbox.

In COD DMZ, players are forced into the war-torn landscape of Hajin, a map disfigured by a nuclear reactor meltdown. It’s a polluted, harsh environment that players can approach in several ways, leaning on mechanics like a Forward Operating Base, crafting, and a bounty system.

In Hajin, players will explore fragments of both Russia and North Korea, as well as a core represented by a portion of South Korea.

Call of Duty: DMZ is Shaping Up

Activision has spent a huge amount of time trying to perfect DMZ, which builds on the four years of beta access opened up in 2022. This new iteration has fresh healing mechanics, more ways to collaborate, a bounty system linked to PvP, tough lieutenants, an improved stash system, and a massive, sprawling, and living map.

It’s a full-fledged game within Modern Warfare 4’s universe, offering a dedicated progression track that allows players to level up and grind based on everything they’re doing in the game.

This year’s DMZ release sees the return of the operator system, again with an evolution. Players can build operators based on styles and approaches, of which there are plenty. There are three core ways to approach DMZ:

  • Story Missions
  • Dynamic Operations
  • Free Roam

In Story Missions, players work through a structured narrative and uncover the mysteries of Hajin. In Dynamic Operations, multi-step objectives that get harder as you go along are the order of the day. Finally, in Free Roam, players can explore Hajin at will, all while digging through a shared world that facilitates both PvP and PvE combat.

This time around, there’s a Forward Operating Base mechanic, a customizable, upgradeable staging ground and social hub that players use to stash their gear, craft new items, and track missions. As Activision has explained, it’s all about exploration and high-risk operations, with players being confronted by unpredictable, emergent enemy forces, some of which are armed to the teeth.

Talking of enemies, DMZ almost has a wanted system that means the more AI combatants you kill, the more you get chased down, especially if you go very loud. But, at the same time, DMZ now has an intuitive stealth system that allows you to capitalize on being sneaky to eliminate enemies without triggering alarms and summoning reinforcements.

All this takes place while the living, breathing world undergoes ongoing transformations. For instance, there’s a graduating weather system that ebbs and flows as you explore the game.

Like before, it’s expected to be a live service game that exists at the very heart of the extraction space.

Call of Duty: DMZ will launch alongside Modern Warfare 4 on October 23rd, 2026. Are you looking forward to the game? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Marvel 1943 has been delayed

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

item.title: Stellar Blade Fans Complain That Blood Rain’s Evie is a ‘Downgrade’
item.content:encoded:
During the Summer Game Fest showcase, Shift Up revealed Stellar Blade: Blood Rain, an all-new standalone title in the universe that debuted with 2024’s Stellar Blade. However, the sensational unveiling of the new game was quickly marred by vocal fans complaining that the new game’s protagonist, Evie, is either a downgrade or looks far too young to be believable.

The comments haven’t been officially addressed, but it’s worth stressing that the first game’s protagonist, Eve, also looked remarkably young when she was first revealed, eventually being shaped into a more mature character with a more ambitious model.

Is Stellar Blade’s Evie Too Young?

One of the most appreciated trailers during this weekend’s Summer Game Fest broadcast was Stellar Blade: Blood Rain. This all-new reveal had fans chomping at the bit, expanding on a universe that first turned heads with the opening game in 2024.

Of course, a lot of the buzz around Stellar Blade came from the existence of the game’s shapely, attractive, and not-afraid-to-flaunt-it main character, known simply as Eve. Shift Up wasted no time in creating dozens of hyper-revealing outfits for Eve, adding a photomode and allowing players to run wild with Eve’s character model.

It landed the team in hot water at one point when they unassumingly reposted NSFW Stellar Blade content in full view of the public eye on Twitter.

Now, with the reveal of Blood Rain, fans of the post-apocalyptic action title have started pulling the new protagonist and the game’s setting to pieces. This time around, we’re assuming control of an angel named Evie, a more lithe, petite, and young-looking character who is, at first glance, a world away from Eve.

I mean, the animations and physics are still there, as are the suggestive, skin-tight outfits, but fans of the first game have taken an issue with just how young and ‘boyish’ Evie looks. It’s easy to see why, as this was one of the first glimpses we had of Evie:

In the trailer (which you can see below), we do learn how powerful Evie is, wielding impressive gauntlets that give her phenomenal hand-to-hand combat abilities. She might be smaller in size than Eve, but she has just as much potential as a warrior in a world that looks war-torn and fractured.

But here’s a comparison, as this is what Eve looked like before and after she was redesigned for the first game, which was released by Shift Up on PlayStation 5 in 2024:

On social media, one user stated that Evie looks like ‘a Korean child’, while another pulled up other images of Evie and stressed that different angles make her look much older, such as this more dramatic shot:

Another user stayed mixed on the topic, writing, ‘The other angles are fairer on her age, but this one looks real childish.’

And of course, some took a more nefarious tact in the discussion: ‘If I found this picture on someone’s hard drive, I would seriously think about reporting them to the authorities.’

There’s no shortage of quips on the topic, and there are plenty of users taking their angles and directly comparing elements of the two characters. One prevalent thing is literally a side-by-side comparison of both Evie and Eve from the perspective of their rears and the size of their breasts.

Gamers.

What do you think? Do you think there’s anything inherently concerning about Stellar Blade: Blood Rain’s Evie, or are you already a fan of the character model? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that a Fallout 3 remaster in Unreal Engine has surfaced

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: Fallout 3 Remastered in Unreal Engine Looks Unbelievable in New Video
item.content:encoded:
Everyone is waiting patiently (and some not so patiently) for the inevitable Fallout 3 remaster. It’s supposed to be coming at some point and might even be revealed or teased during the upcoming XBOX Games Showcase, potentially even alongside a Fallout: New Vegas remaster or remake.

For now, other projects and content injections will need to keep fans satisfied. In a video uploaded recently by one ambitious and mod-savvy creator, we get a look at several minutes of ‘Fallout 3 Remastered’, as it would look if it were brought back to life in Unreal Engine.

Check out the full video below.

Fallout 3 Remastered Video Surfaces Online

Now, I know it’s not the Fallout 3 Remastered, but it’s a Fallout 3 Remastered, and as a die-hard fan, that’s more than enough for me to get excited. I’m a huge advocate for fan-made creations, and whether it’s a short film or a full conversion mod, I’ll happily lap up everything these intelligent creators have to offer.

Recently, a YouTuber by the name of Julian Uccetta uploaded a seven-minute video titled Fallout 3: Unreal – Remastered in Unreal Engine 5, and it’s stunning to behold. From an upgraded UI to better physics, and from clean textures to deeper small details, the effort to create a grassroots remaster is tangible.

Here’s the full video for your enjoyment:

In the description under the video, Uccetta wrote:

This is a very early, very buggy playtest of a full port (and remaster) of Fallout 3 in Unreal Engine 5.7. This was entirely built using a plugin system I am currently working on called ‘Gamebryo Unreal’ which will allow any old Gamebryo Bethesda title from Morrowind to Fallout: New Vegas (and eventually Skyrim: LE and Fallout 4) to easily be ported to Unreal Engine by simply opening the project and selecting the location of the games data.

It was explained that the early development build had ‘a lot of other software running behind it’, which was reflected in some performance issues called out by some early viewers.

What’s more exciting is that fans eager to get their hands on Fallout 3: Unreal can do so right now, as Uccetta has made early test versions available on his Ko-Fi page.

Are you tired of waiting for Bethesda to unveil an official Fallout remaster? Let us know what you’re thinking on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the first teaser trailer for Fallout: Siege of New Vegas

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

item.title: Escape from Tarkov Adaptive Diplomacy Quest Guide—Where Are Ref’s Camps?
item.content:encoded:
Escape from Tarkov’s new Tarkov Hospitality event pits Ref against Jaeger, and it offers players a string of new quests to discover. It all begins with Guardian of Nature, a simple fetch quest that moves on to something more action-based quite quickly.

In Adaptive Diplomacy, players must eliminate PMCs at specific locations on either Woods, Customs, or Shoreline, but those specific locations aren’t immediately obvious.

Read on to learn everything you need to know about Adaptive Diplomacy in Escape from Tarkov.

Where to Find Ref’s Camps in Escape from Tarkov

Adaptive Diplomacy is not a difficult task by any means, but it’s one of those kill quests that requires you to eliminate enemy players (or AI PMCs) in very specific locations. That means that if nobody happens to walk into those locations, you’ll end up frustrated before long.

Fortunately, there are a few locations you can check for Adaptive Diplomacy in this new EFT event.

Ref’s Camp on Woods

Ref’s camp on Woods is located at the EMERCOM Camp alongside the lake, on the route to the Sawmill. It’s the ‘FOB’ location where you find lots of food and medical items, and it’s one of the main hubs of the game.

Ref’s Camp on Customs

Ref’s camp on Customs is located anywhere in the area of Dorms, the duo of buildings along the main road, near the vehicle extract. Again, this is arguably the central hub of Customs, so it shouldn’t take too long to find PMCs.

Ref’s Camp on Shoreline

Ref’s camp on Shoreline is in the top-right of the map, in the Smuggler’s Base location. It’s one of the newest parts of the Shoreline environment, and it’s right in the corner, near a minefield – so be wary!

Don’t Attack the Clean-Up Crew

Be warned that when you head to any of these locations, you’ll see Ref’s ‘clean-up crew’ members in the area, but they’re neutral to PMCs by default. If you eliminate any of them while finding PMCs, you’ll fail this quest immediately.

Just focus on killing PMCs, and before long, you’ll be headed to the next quest, Distributed Strike.

For completing Adaptive Diplomacy, you’ll get:

  • 15,000 EXP
  • 50 GP Coins
  • 1 x AK-74N rifle
  • 2 x AK-74 magazines
  • 1 x BT ammo pack

Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord server if you’ve managed to find the best spot to kill PMCs on Escape from Tarkov.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the new Escape from Tarkov: Arena mode

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

item.title: Escape from Tarkov Guardian of Nature Quest Guide—Where to Find Information on Ref’s Plan
item.content:encoded:
Escape from Tarkov has a new event called Tarkov Hospitality that starts with the Guardian of Nature quest. It’s a simple enough thing that can take just a few minutes to complete, but it requires players to find an item that isn’t immediately obvious. This is another EFT event that sees Ref going up against traders, but this time, he’s warring with Jaeger, and this is where it all begins.

In Guardian of Nature, you need to head to Woods to find information on Ref’s plan, but where do you go, and once you’ve found the location, where do you find the intel?

Read on to get all the answers.

The base requirement for Guardian of Nature, the first quest of the new Escape from Tarkov event, is for players to track down information on Ref’s plan in the EMERCOM Camp on Woods. There are optional objectives, but as you can imagine, you don’t need to do them to move on to the next task.

When you load into a raid on Woods, you’ll need to head to the EMERCOM Camp alongside the lake, on the road to the Sawmill and near the RUAF extraction point. It’s an easy enough location to find, as it’s one of the map’s central loot hubs.

Be warned: you’ll almost definitely find other players here, as well as having a chance of running into Partizan.

Here’s the location:

Once you’re in the camp, you’re looking for a simple document, located atop some medical storage boxes in one of the corners of the camp, near a large, green container. Here’s a screenshot for ease of access:

Once you’ve got that document, you’ll need to successfully extract it from the map to hand in the item to Jaeger and unlock the next quest, Adaptive Diplomacy.

Here are the rewards for finishing Guardian of Nature:

  • 10,000 EXP
  • 100,000 Roubles
  • 1 x M700 Rifle
  • 3 x M700 10-rnd Magazines
  • 2 x M80A1 packs

Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord server if you’ve had any issues solving this new Tarkov event quest.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Tarkov Arena has a new temporary mode

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

item.title: All Confirmed & Rumored Games For XBOX Games Showcase 2026
item.content:encoded:
Summer Game Fest is in full swing, and with Sony’s State of Play and the main SGF Showcase out of the way, all eyes are beginning to move towards the XBOX Games Showcase 2026.

This year, it’s looking like a two-parter, with the XBOX-focused showcase kicking things off before we get a Gears of War: E-Day Direct, which, as you might have guessed, will give us a deep look into the highly anticipated prequel.

Gears of War aside, though, what else can we expect to see during the XBOX Games Showcase? We’re here to answer that question, as we’ve collected a list of every confirmed and rumored game for the XBOX Games Showcase 2026—check them all out below.

Every Confirmed Game For XBOX Games Showcase

Whilst it’s obvious that Gears of War: E-Day will appear during the XBOX Games Showcase, a Steam banner seems to have confirmed the appearance of seven titles during the event.

On Steam, you can find a section displaying games announced at each of the Summer Game Fest events, and the XBOX Games Showcase has a banner. Eagle-eyed fan curatorgamingx on X spotted that some titles were already displayed here. It’s now been updated to remove these games, but the following seven titles were present:

  • Fable
  • Halo Campaign Evolved
  • Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4
  • State of Decay 3
  • Minecraft Dungeons 2
  • Clockwork Revolution
  • Gears of War: E-Day

None of these titles are especially surprising, and it’s fair to say each of them was expected. With Fable’s recent delay, a new look should tide fans over, whilst a release date for Halo Campaign Evolved seems well overdue, especially with the recent leaks.

  • More Insider Gaming: Summer Game Fest 2026 Showcase—Everything Announced

Speaking of leaks, Minecraft Dungeons 2 pre-emptively appeared on the Nintendo eStore, revealing its release date and price—expect an official announcement this Sunday. Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4 should also make an appearance, with a new look at the returning DMZ extraction game mode on the cards.

As for Clockwork Revolution, a release date could be a real possibility, with many fans eagerly awaiting the blend of Dishonoured and Bioshock action. And then there’s State of Decay 3. The game has been in development for a long time now, but with the game ‘coming on well,’ it would make sense to see some more of the game—and maybe a release window?

Every Rumored Game For XBOX Games Showcase

Aside from those heavy hitters mentioned above, there’s still room for plenty of surprises at the XBOX Games Showcase. Here’s what else we think has a strong chance of appearing based on rumors and speculation.

Persona 4 Revival

Persona 4 Revival was revealed at last year’s XBOX Games Showcase, and with the game being rated in Korea, it’s likely we’re nearing a release date announcement. Rumors suggest it’s due next February, but we could find out for sure on Sunday.

Persona 6

Persona 4 Revival is bound to be great, but it’s fair to say that everyone is more than ready for the next mainline entry into the franchise. Well, it doesn’t seem like we should have to wait much longer, as according to leakers, Persona 6 is set to be unveiled at the XBOX Games Showcase.

  • More Insider Gaming: Sony State of Play June 2026 Showcase—Everything Announced

With SEGA issuing copyright strikes to Persona 6 leakers, it’s fair to assume they’re preparing for the big reveal. It might still be a ways away, but even an official announcement will set the fandom alight—is anyone else desperate to hear just a slice of the new soundtrack?!

Metro 2039

With such a captivating announcement back in April, it’s high time we see some proper gameplay for Metro 2039. It’s currently slated for a late 2026 release, so it wouldn’t be out of the question, and based on what we’ve seen so far, I’m more than ready for more.

Beast of Reincarnation

Beast of Reincarnation, arguably Game Freak’s most ambitious non-Pokémon title, has appeared at a variety of events. It was announced at an XBOX showcase, though a release date was shared during the February State of Play. It’s slated for a day-one release on Game Pass, so perhaps it’ll share some new gameplay ahead of its launch.

Blade

It’s been almost three years since Blade was announced at the 2023 Game Awards, and there has been almost radio silence since. With his MCU movie trapped in limbo, fans of the Daywalker are desperate to at least see the game get acknowledged, let alone a release date or gameplay.

Fallout 3 Remake/Something Bethesda

Look, I’m sure we’re all desperate to hear news regarding the Elder Scrolls 6, but let’s be real, that game is likely still years away. In the meantime, though, how’s about that long-rumored Fallout 3 Remake? Fans have been speculating about this game for a good while now, and an official announcement has to arrive at some point—why not Sunday?

Wolfenstein 3

It’s been over a decade since the new Wolfenstein trilogy began, with Wolfenstein: The New Order. With rumors that the third game in the trilogy is in development, could we see its official announcement at the XBOX Games Showcase? There’s certainly a strong chance.

Other Potential Appearances

There are bound to be plenty of surprises during the XBOX Games Showcase, and it’s impossible to predict them all. That said, here are a few more titles that could appear at the event, including some that were recently added to XBOX Game Pass Coming Soon:

  • Resonance: A Plague Tale Legacy
  • Tropico 7
  • There Are No Ghosts At The Grand
  • Armatus
  • Witchbrook
  • Mudang: Two Hearts
  • Spyro
  • Forza Horizon 6 Update/PS5 Release Date
  • Diablo 4

Are you excited for the XBOX Games Showcase this year? Do you think we’re missing any potential reveals? If so, let us know in the comments below, or share your pre-show predictions in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Insider Gaming content, check out the following:

  • Insider Gaming Showcase—Everything Announced
  • Xbox Games Showcase 2026 & Gears of War: E-Day Direct—Date, Start Times & How to Watch
  • Best Sony PlayStation Days of Play 2026 Deals: End Date & Sale Games

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Tupac Shakur’s Stranger Than Heaven Reveal Earned an Immense Crowd Reaction
item.content:encoded:
Last night, RGG and SEGA took to the stage at Summer Game Fest in Los Angeles to unveil more of Stranger Than Heaven, an all-new action-adventure title with gritty, combat-laced subtones. As the trailer played out, viewers learned more about the Like a Dragon prequel, but things took a surprising turn when a familiar-looking face emerged from a doorway.

Tupac Shakur, the iconic artist and actor tragically gunned down in 1996 at just 25 years of age, will appear in RGG’s Stranger Than Heaven, alongside Snoop Dogg, who was revealed several weeks ago. His character is named Amaru, and while the capacity of his presence hasn’t been confirmed, SEGA has confirmed that AI wasn’t used to bring him to life.

Tupac Generates Immense Hype at Summer Game Fest

When RGG unveiled Tupac’s presence in Stranger Than Heaven, the roof was blown off the Dolby Theatre in Los Angeles. Nobody saw the move coming, and the crowd became even more electric when none other than Snoop Dogg marched onto the stage to talk about Tupac’s imminent arrival in the action title.

Here’s the full segment:

Speaking about Tupac, Snoop said:

The Tupac estate, my son, and myself, we work very closely together, so it just makes sense to put him in this game, because his likeness and his spirit still lives on. I just feel like it was so connected to what we’re doing.

Snoop also took a moment to talk about Death Row Games, teasing a little more about the studio that was kind of unveiled a good couple of years ago, but has been mostly silent since.

We’re building a hip-hop gaming universe, bringing an authentic culture to life. We’ve just got a lot of plans that we plan on bringing to life in a couple of months.

SEGA deliberately assured fans that Tupac Shakur has not been brought back to life in RGG’s Stranger Than Heaven via generative AI, promising the ‘utmost respect for his legacy’ while making him a hugely impactful part of the game’s hype train.

Being in the crowd at Summer Game Fest, I can attest to the pop that rang out to the rafters when an older, reimagined Tupac was brought faithfully back into the fold in an all-new and wholesome way. It’s not yet clear if Tupac’s character, Amaru, will be voiced, but more will be revealed soon.

Stranger Than Heaven will be released on January 15, 2027. Are you picking up a copy? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that ARC Raiders’ player count continues to slide

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: ARC Raiders Player Count Has Been Bleeding For Five Months and Isn’t Slowing
item.content:encoded:
It has been more than seven months since ARC Raiders hit the extraction shooter space, bringing about an all-new concept in the genre and introducing millions of people to the notion of escaping. Embark Studios made bank from this sensational game, pushing out content and keeping things fresh while retaining players, but the situation started going south fairly quickly.

In the last five months, ARC Raiders has seen a drastic drop in players, with each successive major update failing to capture the attention and interest of gamers worldwide. The copies have been sold, and the millions have been made, but with each new month, ARC Raiders dwindles further down the charts.

And as we all know, that’s never good news for a live service game with ‘years of content’ in the pipeline.

The Degradation of ARC Raiders is Tangible

If we go by Steam’s player numbers, which is the only accurate and publicly accessible data out there, we can see a dire picture being painted. In November 2025, during the game’s first full month on the market, ARC Raiders hit lofty peaks, securing a top player count on Steam of 481,966 users.

The next month saw a sizeable dip, but that’s to be expected, with early adopters who don’t quite enjoy the game enough inevitably dropping off during the holiday season while they play other games.

But when the new year came around, a minor uptick hit in January, only to be followed by months of consistent downward trending for ARC Raiders.

At the worst, ARC Raiders saw a -39% drop month on month in peak player count. As the months have pushed on, the percentages have eased off, but that’s because the stabilization is coming from the core community holding on in the hopes that more content will come soon.

Here’s how the data looks for ARC Raiders’ player count drop in the last few months (using peak player numbers from Steam):

  • January 2026: 466,372
  • February 2026: 375,294 (-19.5%)
  • March 2026: 252,050 (-33%)
  • April 2026: 154,498 (-39%)
  • May 2026: 122,157 (-21%)

June has already started with a -9.5% drop, with a peak of 110,659 being secured, but at the time of writing, ARC Raiders was sitting at an all-time low of just 29,688 active players.

What’s Wrong with ARC Raiders?

There’s not much that’s inherently wrong with ARC Raiders, but there are a few things that have pushed players in the opposite direction in recent months. This is visible by taking a cursory glance at the game’s review history and seeing just how many days negative reviews have been outpacing positive reviews:

Put simply, a two-fold thing kicked in to push players away.

The most recent major updates, including Shrouded Sky and Riven Tides, simply weren’t as ambitious or exciting as players were hoping for. They failed to deliver, and that left an impact on the community. Then, Embark Studios emerged and said that the next major update wouldn’t be dropping until October.

The team said that they couldn’t sustain the cadence of updates they were maintaining, and instead, they’d opt to roll out just two major updates a year.

Combine that with some bizarre choices around weapon balancing and durability, PvP vs. PvE content, and a lack of innovation in the ARC enemy space, and you’ve got a game that’s working its way down the charts relatively quickly.

Of course, it doesn’t help that streamers have long since abandoned ARC Raiders en masse, moving on to greener pastures. That’s one of the hardest-hitting areas where ARC Raiders has all but imploded in the last few months. When content and streams were booming, ARC Raiders was flying high, but that’s a thing of the past.

At the time of writing, just 8,000 people were watching ARC Raiders content, compared to an all-time peak of almost 400,000 viewers.

Also, we’ve seen other competing games come up into the fold a little more. Marathon recently had a major update that introduced some PvE content, Escape from Tarkov has a new map, and Call of Duty fans started getting excited about the reveal of DMZ.

Escape from Tarkov is retaining players better than any other extraction shooter out there, but it’s worth stressing it has pioneered the genre for the last decade, so that’s almost expected.

For now, all eyes in the ARC Raiders space are fixed firmly on October’s Frozen Trail update, and whether it’ll revitalize the game to any meaningful standard, just a year down the line.

Are you still playing ARC Raiders? Do you think we should be concerned about the player count drop? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that a new Stellar Blade has been revealed

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.
